Tonight's guest was featured on episode 613.
Because of all the details he had to share about his encounter, though,
we ran out of time and didn't have time for me to ask all the questions I had for him.
So with that in mind, I asked him if he'd be interested in coming back to do a Q&A,
and luckily he agreed to do that.

segments.text: Thank you.
Thank you.
If you'd like to be able to listen to the show without ads,
and have full access to bonus content, that's an option.
To find out how, please go to dogmanencounters.com forward slash podcast.
Tonight's guest was featured on episode 613.
Because of all the details he had to share about his encounter, though,
we ran out of time and didn't have time for me to ask all the questions I had for him.
So with that in mind, I asked him if he'd be interested in coming back to do a Q&A,
and luckily he agreed to do that.
So thanks again so much for coming back, Josh.
I really appreciate it.
Hey, no problem, Vic.
And glad to be here once again.
Well, it's great having you back.
Josh, for anyone who missed episode 613, please give us a flyover of the details of that encounter to bring them up to speed.
Well, it was the opening day of rifle season in Michigan.
And I had went up to my camp.
and I had walked out to go to my deer blind before it was even light out
and I had a few noises on the way there.
It's in a cedar swamp and all that, but nothing major.
I got there, waited for it to get light out.
Pretty quick, I would say, I noticed there was a doe there
and I'm just looking down a lane.
And the rest is all swamp and trees, cedar trees you can't really see in there.
And so I'm looking at that and almost immediately I'm hearing what I think is a buck walking to the lane because they're in rut this time of year.
well that went on for quite some time and
i don't think what came out made those footsteps but out of nowhere a massive looking wolf
i mean it looked pretty similar just to a regular wolf but i mean more like the size of a horse
and it's not that far away and it stepped out in front of that doe
I immediately thought that was a dogman type creature just because of the size
and I will say that I have listened to these shows and stuff and that's kind of what gave
me that knowledge and stuff of these creatures anyway but so that thing just stood there for a
little bit and it went off and I sat there and thought about it. I began to calm down a bit
because it didn't seem like some evil creature and I don't think it was, but I wasn't ready to
walk out yet either. And I just had to gather my thoughts. I've never seen anything like that.
Well, it was shortly after that, I noticed that either the same deer or another deer was there.
I think it was the same one.
And beside that, but more towards me,
this black creature, I guess it stood up,
I'm not too sure.
To me it looked like it just formed up really quick
and it was all black and this immediately to me was a dog man I could see the pointed ears it faced away from me pretty quickly and I could see the muscles on his back shoulders stuff
like that it walked away from me and made a swatting motion at the doe I
could see its long claws I don't think it actually hit the doe but I don't know
where that doe went from there. And then basically, well, it turned around, looked at me once, and then
back, and then kept walking, and it jumped high up into a tree, and then it went and pounced off of
that onto a limb that was overhanging the lane, and it appeared to just lay down on its belly and
grasp that limb, like hug it with its arms and its back legs, and now no matter what I did with my
scope. I mean, I couldn't see where its head was. I can just see there's a ball there, you know, and
yeah, it was shortly after that. I watched that for a good while, decided to walk out of there. It was
terrifying to walk out. I did hear something following me. I stopped. It got to be too much.
I stopped and yelled at it. I got my rifle off safety and, you know, just told it, please don't
step out or any of that just stop I'm leaving if you step out in front of me I might shoot because
I'm on the verge of panic and running and oh just thinking about it but um you know I did make it
stopped and I did make it back to the camp and that pretty much would sum up a short version
of what happened without too many details really and that went together to make for one heck of a
long morning for you. Yeah, it really did. And I don't know if I said before, but when I, after I
got back to camp, my dad shot a deer. So here I got to go right back in the woods, but I mean a
different section of the woods and with my dad, but yeah, so it was all a rough morning doing all
that. Well, I'm sure it was. And after your dad shot that one, at least you're with him, like you
said you weren't alone so that's better than your past situation i'm not calling you a liar not by
a long shot josh but that does staying put when that giant wolf walked out in front of it just
doesn't make sense to me at all deer just don't act that way when something like that happens
do you have any thoughts on why it acted the way it did that i missed yeah i do have a few
and I mean like I did say you know after that big wolf left it's not like I seen that doe right away
I was kind of lost in thought or whatever but at some point I noticed not that I really seen
movement but I'm looking through my scope and thinking and I see it there or what looks like
a doe maybe the same one I'm thinking just because of the way it looks and I did I was really trying
to look like, okay, am I, you know, is that actually a doe?
Or like, because so many things can look like deer down that lane with all the mess.
But it wasn't moving, but here's my thoughts on that.
Now, when all this is happening, I did mention that I felt those vibrations in the blind
and in my feet, and maybe even like up to my knees a little bit.
So, I guess my thoughts on the situation, even though it does seem crazy, but when I heard walking up that I thought was a buck coming for that doe, I don't think was that first big wolf creature.
now that I've thought about it I think it was possibly that black creature dogman like creature
but walking on two feet and this other creature maybe or whatever it was I think something
basically used infrasound is what I'm trying to say and it maybe also hit that doe
um is really the only time I seen that doe move is when it was out there we both heard movement
and it turned its head.
Now, I guess that's a theory I've come to
because I've heard other stories about this happening to deer
and they're just frozen still.
Now, I don't even know if it could see that huge wolf creature.
That could have been possibly a spirit.
I'm not sure, Vic.
My thoughts are all jumbled on all of it.
Now, the last thing I'll say,
is there a chance when I notice that doe again
and it wasn't moving,
am I 100 positive that there was a doe there I pretty sure but not 100 because of what going on in my mind And fairly shortly there that black creature

That's really all I can say about that.
If that dogman was there long before you ever thought it was,
that definitely would make sense if it hit that doe with infrasound.
that would cause it to freeze or it could cause it to freeze like that.

but that would definitely explain it.

You know, I'm open to,
was that like an infrasound weapon that hit me
and it's making me see funny things?
I mean, that sounds a little bit crazy,
but I'm open to anything.
I don't know what happened.
Well, Infrasound can make you do that. So that's another good explanation.
If you've had a dogman encounter and would like to speak with me about it,
whether in private or on the show, please go to dogmanencounters.com and submit a report.
Just out of curiosity, Josh, why do you hunt from a blind on the ground instead of a tree stand?
Um, well, for a couple reasons. Um, when my dad and grandpa built the camp in, uh,
I believe it was 91.
It might have been 1990.
But either way, my dad built that blind out there, and he cut those lanes.
You've got to imagine this thick, really thick cedar swamp.
I mean, there's nowhere you could walk in there.
You'd have to cut a whole big section if you wanted to get up in a tree and be able to bow hunt or shoot a rifle from there.
but mainly it's just because that's the blind and that's uh what was put there and was there for me
to hunt um as i got older you know and i mean that is the main reason you know my dad cut those lanes
years ago put the blind there and that's just where me and my little brother grew up hunting and
I've never really hunted anywhere else my whole life.
Yeah, that makes really good sense too.
Yeah, it's much easier to clear lanes on the ground at ground level than to have to climb up and trim branches above ground level.
So, yeah, like I said, that makes really good sense.
It sounds to me like that path you had to walk down to get to your blind was pretty darn claustrophobic.
How much shoulder room would you say you have on either side of you as you're walking down that path between the dense brush on either side?
Um, you know, maybe five, six feet.
I mean, you could say this, like I could carry my rifle sideways if I needed to.
Um, but you would never walk shoulder to shoulder.
If me and my dad or brother were going down there, it'd always be one after the other.
Because it is very thin, and there's so many ups and downs and stumps, and, you know, it's nearly impossible to get a four-wheeler back there.
So, I've never measured it, but it is, that's all there is, is that little lane to get there.
And, yeah, it's not too wide.
Yeah, I'd say it wasn't.
Yeah, that sounds like it's pretty tight to me.

And for anyone who's never been in a cedar forest or a cedar swamp, the needles that cedar trees drop, that makes it really easy to walk around without making too much noise if you don't want to make noise.
So that right there makes it even more intense to be in that situation.
I feel for you.
I really do.
When you got to the blind that morning, how long did you have to wait before sunup?
Oh, let's see.
well, I was, I did get there a little later than I wanted to, but it was still dark,
but after letting my eyes adjust, you know, maybe after five minutes,
five, ten minutes, it's starting to get light, and then it's slowly getting light from there,
because I want to say I left to go to the blind around 5.45 a.m.,
And then if I'm getting there, let's say between like 6 and 6, 10 a.m., I mean, just a guess, a person could look up, you know, what time the sun's coming up in Michigan that time of year.

But I might be a little off but I do know as I got there settled it slowly seems like it already starting to get light after a little bit So you didn have to wait all that long then No That good Thank goodness for that considering what you experienced
Not all that long after that.
When you made it to the blind, you heard movement around you, you said.
How close did that movement seem to be to you, and exactly how long would you say it was that that movement started after you sat down in the blind?
actually when i sat down in the blind i was already hearing movement in the woods
which to me just was maybe there's bucks chasing does around right now you know which this isn't

i got it ready i got the safety off i'm really pushing it down um

Yeah, that makes sense.
Were those subjects that you heard moving around you in a circular way or were they
